rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by attaching any generated annex only to its owning declared component or implementation scope.
Keep type-scope contracts separate from implementation-scope content; do not move scope-specific content across owners during fusion.
Do not introduce references that are invalid in the selected owner scope, especially implementation-only paths in type-owned content.
generic_rules: Maintain existing structural elements, ordering, and non-target content while merging new annex material.
If an annex already exists on the owner, extend or reconcile that annex rather than altering unrelated model sections.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is enclosed in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Implementation-level content belongs in implementation scope after relevant subcomponents and connections when such dependencies exist.
anti_patterns: Do not attach content to a different owner than the intended component or implementation.
Do not restructure connections, features, or subcomponents as part of annex insertion.
Do not rely on type assumptions when fusing implementation-scoped content, or implementation-only references when fusing type-scoped content.
irrelevant_topics: Temporal equation patterns and logical operator selection for clause generation.
Scalar helper type declarations and expression typing details for generated AGREE code.
Requirement-specific boolean condition semantics beyond owner/scope preservation during fusion.
